user_query:
I’m organizing small neighborhood meet-and-greets for Rep. Jean Philippe Barros and need help picking one meeting time per precinct that will maximize attendance based on the files I’ve compiled. Please:

1) Read input/supporter_availability.csv and input/venues.json.

2) For each precinct present in supporter_availability.csv, schedule exactly one meetup by choosing a venue and a single time slot that is:
   - In the same precinct as the supporters and the venue (venue.precinct must match).
   - Listed in the venue’s available_slots.
   - Maximizing the number of supporters in that precinct whose available_slots include that slot.
   - Not exceeding the venue’s capacity (assigned_count ≤ capacity). Use the slot/venue combination that maximizes min(raw_available_count, capacity). If there’s a tie, break ties by slot order ["Tue 6-7pm", "Wed 6-7pm", "Thu 6-7pm", "Sat 10-11am"], then by venue_id ascending.

3) Assign supporters to the chosen meeting for their precinct if their availability includes the chosen slot. If there are more available supporters than capacity, select which supporters are assigned by sorting their email addresses ascending and taking the first up to capacity. All other supporters in that precinct should be marked unassigned.

4) Write the following output files:
   - output/meetings.csv with columns: precinct, venue_id, venue_name, address, slot, capacity, available_supporters_count, assigned_count.
   - output/assignments.csv with columns: supporter_email, supporter_name, precinct, assigned (yes/no), venue_id, slot. For unassigned supporters, set assigned to "no" and leave venue_id and slot empty.
   - output/status_update.md: a concise status update addressed to our volunteer coordinators that mentions Rep. Jean Philippe Barros by name and includes:
     • A short overview paragraph about the purpose (coordinating neighborhood meet-and-greets for supporters).
     • A section per precinct summarizing the selected venue, slot, expected turnout (assigned_count/capacity), and total number of remaining unassigned supporters.
     • A closing note suggesting whether additional sessions might be needed if unassigned supporters remain.

All outputs should be derived from and consistent with the inputs. Don’t invent additional data. Ensure deterministic choices using the tie-breaking and email sorting rules above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
